data.title: Japan is said to rule out using Treasuries as leverage in tariff talks
data.description:
Japan has no intention of leveraging its holdings of U.S. government debt as a bargaining chip in upcoming trade negotiations with Washington, Bloomberg News reported on Sunday.

Itsunori Onodera, the policy chief of Japan‚Äôs ruling Liberal Democratic Party, emphasized that as a key U.S. ally, Japan would avoid any actions that could disrupt financial markets.

‚ÄúIt‚Äôs not in our interest to take steps that would destabilize U.S. Treasury markets,‚Äù he was quoted as saying on national broadcaster NHK.

His remarks come amid heightened market sensitivity. U.S. Treasury yields jumped sharply last week, marking their biggest surge since the onset of the COVID-19 pandemic, as investors speculated over possible shifts in foreign demand for U.S. debt. Concerns have grown that major reserve holders, including China, might be reassessing their exposure due to the Trump administration‚Äôs aggressive trade posture.

Japan is currently seeking an exemption from the sweeping U.S. tariffs that took effect on April 9. Meanwhile, Washington is pressing Tokyo for greater market access for American agricultural exports and liquefied natural gas. The tariffs have hit Japan with a 24% rate, and its vital auto sector now faces a 25% duty.

Onodera also called for Japan to raise the tariff issue at the World Trade Organization and expressed solidarity with neighboring countries in Asia, many of which have been subjected to even steeper U.S. tariffs. He added that Japan aims to deepen regional coordination through the Association of Southeast Asian Nations, Bloomberg News reported.

Dear Readers:¬ÝWe recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to¬Ýclick here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Earnings week ahead: BAC, GS, JNJ, TSM, UNH, ABT, UAL, C, NFLX and more
data.description:
Earnings season picks up pace in the upcoming holiday-shortened week with a lineup of heavyweights across banking, healthcare, tech, and industrials.¬Ý

Key names set to report include Bank of America (BAC), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX), Taiwan Semiconductor (NYSE:TSM), and Citigroup (NYSE:C).¬Ý

Other closely watched updates will come from ASML (ASML), Kinder Morgan (NYSE:KMI), UnitedHealth (UNH), Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS), Blackstone (BX), Abbott Labs (NYSE:ABT), American Express (AXP), United Airlines (UAL), Alcoa (AA), U.S. Bancorp (USB), and Prologis (PLD), offering a broad look at macro and sector-specific trends heading into the heart of the season.

Below is a rundown of major quarterly updates anticipated in the week of April 14 to April 18:

Monday, April 14

Following a series of bank earnings reported at the end of last week,¬ÝGoldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) is scheduled to post its Q1 results before Monday‚Äôs opening bell. Analysts project a 6% Y/Y EPS increase and a 4% rise in revenue.

While Wall Street maintains a Buy rating on the stock, Seeking Alpha‚Äôs Quant Rating system holds a more cautious Hold due to concerns around valuation and profitability.

Last month, Oppenheimer downgraded the stock to Perform from Outperform, citing a stalled M&A rebound amid tariff uncertainty and broader macro risks.

Similarly, SA contributor Daniel Urbina recently downgraded Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) to Sell from Buy, citing a shift in the macroeconomic outlook since February. He noted that recession concerns and weaker economic growth could weigh on IPOs, M&A activity, and fee income. While valuations remain near historical averages, Urbina argues that the stock doesn‚Äôt reflect the risk of a sharper slowdown, making the current pricing less compelling.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $12.28
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $14.71B
  • Earnings Insight: Goldman Sachs has beaten revenue expectations in 7 of the past 8 quarters, missing EPS estimates twice in that span.

Also reporting:¬ÝM&T Bank (MTB), Ontrak (OTRK), Pinnacle Financial Partners (PNFP), and more.

Tuesday, April 15

Bank of America (BAC)

Bank of America (BAC) is set to report its Q1 earnings before Tuesday‚Äôs market open, alongside Citigroup (NYSE:C), with analysts expecting modest single-digit year-over-year growth in both earnings and revenue. The results will follow a wave of Q1 reports from peers, including upbeat numbers from JPMorgan (JPM) and Morgan Stanley (MS), and mixed outcomes from BlackRock (BLK) and Wells Fargo (WFC), a lineup that has broadly improved sentiment across the banking sector.

The bank gets a Hold rating from Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system, in contrast to the Strong Buy consensus among Wall Street analysts.

SA contributor Julia Ostian maintains a Hold rating on Bank of America, citing heightened economic uncertainty and a shift in market sentiment heading into the Q1 2025 release. While BAC delivered a solid Q4 2024 performance, the stock has notably lagged peers like JPMorgan and Wells Fargo this year, raising questions about its resilience. Although management remains optimistic about net interest income (NII) growth in 2025, Ostian warns that inflationary pressures, tariff concerns, and rising consumer credit risks could test that upbeat outlook. She advises a cautious stance until the earnings report provides updated clarity.¬Ý

On the other hand, SA contributor Paul Franke adopts a more bearish stance on Bank of America, assigning it a¬ÝSell rating. He warns that the bank's sizable exposure to consumer and small business loans leaves it particularly vulnerable amid growing macroeconomic pressures. Franke also flags Warren Buffett‚Äôs decision to trim his BAC holdings in 2024 as a concerning signal. Looking ahead, he cautions that a potential 2025 recession‚Äîpossibly fueled by stagflationary policies and a pullback in Wall Street wealth, could significantly pressure the stock. Additionally, his technical momentum analysis, including weak readings from On-Balance Volume (OBV) and Ease of Movement (EMV), suggests the risk of further downside.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $0.81
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $26.91B
  • Earnings Insight: The bank has exceeded EPS expectations in 6 of the past 8 quarters, and revenue expectations in only one of those reports.

Also reporting:¬ÝJohnson & Johnson (JNJ), Citigroup (NYSE:C), United Airlines (UAL), Ericsson (ERIC), PNC Financial Services Group (PNC), Omnicom Group (OMC), Interactive Brokers Group (IBKR), J.B. Hunt Transport Services (JBHT), Albertsons Companies (ACI), and more.

Wednesday, April 16

Abbott Laboratories (NYSE:ABT)

Healthcare conglomerate Abbott Laboratories (NYSE:ABT) is scheduled to report its Q1 financial results before the market opens on Wednesday.

Recently, the company secured FDA approval for an investigational device exemption tied to its Coronary Intravascular Lithotripsy System, aimed at treating severe calcification in coronary arteries before stenting. The clinical trial is expected to enroll up to 335 participants across 47 sites in the U.S.-

Meanwhile, Abbott was recently named among the 30 most preferred stocks across all 11 S&P sectors by the UBS CIO Americas wealth management equity sector strategy team.

Abbott receives a Hold rating from Seeking Alpha‚Äôs Quant Rating system, in contrast to a consensus Buy rating from Wall Street analysts.

SA contributor Motti Sapir takes a bullish stance on ABT, arguing that it is undervalued due to strong fundamentals, robust cash flow, and strategic moves in high-growth areas like diabetes and weight-loss care. The company‚Äôs FreeStyle Libre system and new Protality brand support its long-term growth trajectory. With a net income margin of nearly 32% and over $8.5B in operational cash flow, Sapir assigns a 12‚Äì18-month price target of $145‚Äì$160, calling Abbott a compelling opportunity for growth, stability, and income.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $1.07
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $10.41B
  • Earnings Insight: The company has exceeded EPS and revenue expectations in 7 of the past 8 quarters.

Also reporting: Kinder Morgan (NYSE:KMI), ASML Holding (ASML), Alcoa (AA), U.S. Bancorp (USB), CSX (CSX), Prologis (PLD), Travelers Companies (TRV), Citizens Financial Group (CFG), SL Green Realty (SLG), Bank OZK (OZK), Progressive (PGR), Martin Midstream Partners LP (MMLP), and more.

Thursday, April 17

Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX)

Streaming giant Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) is set to report Q1 results on Thursday after the bell. Shares have climbed 49% over the past 12 months, but are up only 3% YTD.

The Seeking Alpha Quant Rating turned cautious in April, downgrading the stock to Hold from Strong Buy, even as Wall Street analysts maintain Buy ratings.

SA contributor YR Research remains bullish, citing Netflix‚Äôs transformation into a ‚ÄúNeed‚Äù over a ‚ÄúWant‚Äù with over 300M subscribers, an unmatched content library, and a leading digital platform. The contributor‚Äôs proprietary data points to 8% viewership growth in Q1, alongside record-high Nielsen viewing share, supporting expectations of solid subscriber and revenue gains.

While acknowledging the stock‚Äôs premium valuation, YR Research believes consensus estimates are conservative, assigning a $1,000 price target based on a 33x multiple on projected 2026 earnings.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $5.74
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $10.51B
  • Earnings Insight: Netflix has exceeded EPS estimates in 7 of the past 8 quarters, while exceeding revenue expectations in just 6 of those reports.

Also reporting:¬ÝTaiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (NYSE:TSM), UnitedHealth Group (UNH), Blackstone (BX), American Express (AXP), Truist Financial Corporation (TFC), D.R. Horton (DHI), Charles Schwab (SCHW), Huntington Bancshares (HBAN), KeyCorp (KEY), Regions Financial (RF), Infosys Technologies (INFY), Ally Financial (ALLY), Fifth Third Bancorp (FITB), and more.

Friday, April 18

Good Friday, the U.S. markets will be closed.

data.title: Plunging oil prices force U.S. oil industry to consider cutting production, jobs
data.description:
Despite President Trump's exhortations to "drill, baby, drill," U.S. shale oil producers are facing their most serious crisis since the pandemic, as the sudden oil price selloff triggered in part by President Trump's trade war has pushed parts of the sector¬Ýto the brink of failure, industry executives warned this week.

Oil markets have been hit with a potential "double whammy" of declining demand from a tariff-induced economic slowdown combined with new supplies, with OPEC+ announcing last week it planned to raise production faster than expected in the coming months.

Analysts say Trump's decision to leave tariffs on China, the world's biggest oil importer, will continue to loom over global crude demand prospects.

The U.S. Energy Information Administration this week sharply cut its estimate of U.S. crude prices to $63.88/bbl for 2025 from a prior forecast of $70.68/bb, and lowered its forecast for global oil consumption growth for this year by 400K bbl/day to 900K bbl/day.

Tariffs also will make it more expensive to buy steel and equipment, which could further discourage drilling unless oil prices rise substantially.

Benchmark U.S. crude has fallen nearly 14% so far this month, leaving the price below the level many producers in Texas say they need to break even and even causing concern that the industry could be forced to idle rigs.

Rystad Energy said this week that many U.S. shale producers faced breakeven costs of ~$62/bbl when debt servicing and dividend payments were included, and "in reality, even a company operating on $40 breakeven acreage would be inclined to slow down activity when prices fall below $65/bbl, as their level of dividend coverage would be at risk," Rystad said.

Energy prices remain well below levels producers say they need before they substantially increase activity and will not get there any time soon, according to the Federal Reserve Bank of Kansas City's latest quarterly survey, which said the energy firms surveyed need to see oil at $85/bbl and natural gas at $5.10/MMBtu before they significantly ramp up drilling.

"If oil does go into lower $60s or upper $50s, public independents that are already capital disciplined are going to have to cut their budget and cut rigs," said Bryan Sheffield, founder of energy investors Formentera Partners and former Parsely Energy CEO.

"If prices get sub-$60 and stay there, we'll see a definite drop in the rig count," Marauder Capital managing partner Roe Patterson said. "They've definitely opened the door for the OPEC countries to gain market share here, and it's an inadvertent, self-inflicted wound."

While it may cost under $40/bbl to drill in the best parts of the Permian Basin, new well drilling in North Dakota would require oil prices at ~$57/bbl, according to Wood Mackenzie, meaning operations in those basins would be more at risk at current price levels.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:XLE), (NYSEARCA:XOP), (AMLP), (VDE), (OIH), (IXC), (CRAK)

data.title: KKR nearing deal to acquire OSTTRA for around $3B: report
data.description:
KKR (NYSE:KKR) is close to a deal to acquire post-trade services provider OSTTRA for around $3B, Bloomberg reported Saturday, citing people familiar with the matter.¬Ý

According to the report, KKR‚Äôs talks for the joint venture of CME Group (CME) and S&P Global (SPGI) are in the advanced stages after it beat out other possible buyers, the people said.¬Ý

A transaction could be announced as soon as next week, the people were quoted as saying.¬Ý

GTCR, Advent International, CVC Capital Partners (OTC:CVCCF) and other private-equity firms had been after OSTTR, Bloomberg said.¬Ý

Negotiations are continuing and a deal may be delayed or fall through, the people reportedly said.¬Ý

KKR, OSTTRA, CME Group, S&P Global, GTCR, Advent International, and CVC Capital Partner did not immediately respond to requests for comment by Seeking Alpha

data.title: Trump exempts smartphones, computers, chips from reciprocal tariffs
data.description:
Update 4:25 PM ET: Adds details, Rewrites throughout¬Ý

President Donald Trump's administration on Friday exempted smartphones, computers and other electronics mostly imported from China from reciprocal tariffs, providing major relief for American tech players like Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL), Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA)¬Ýand Dell (NYSE:DELL).¬Ý

The exclusions, published by the U.S. Customs and Border Protection agency, follow Trump's moves to impose 145% tariffs on products from China and 10% baseline tariffs on almost every other nation.¬Ý

The 20 categories listed in the notice are exempt from 125% tariffs on Chinese imports and 10% baseline tariffs on other countries. Goods from China will still face a 20% tariff, a levy imposed to force Beijing to crack down on fentanyl.¬Ý

The exemptions are applicable to popular electronics items such as¬Ýsmartphones, laptops, computers, computer processors, memory chips, flat panel TV displays, solar cells, hard drives.¬Ý

The guidance should provide some respite to smartphone manufacturers like Apple (AAPL) and Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF), AI major Nvidia (NVDA), server makers like Super Micro (NASDAQ:SMCI) and Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE), computer makers like Dell (DELL) and HP (NYSE:HPQ), and chip major¬ÝTaiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE:TSM).¬Ý

Tech bull Dan Ives, who slashed price targets¬Ýfor Magnificent 7 names like Apple (AAPL) and Tesla (TSLA) over Trump's tariffs, called the exclusions "the best news possible for tech investors."¬Ý

"Big Tech is back off the cliff with these exemptions and this changes the entire situation for tech stocks with this black swan event for the industry removed," said Ives. "The U.S. tech industry has a loud voice and despite initial strong pushback against exemptions within the White House the reality of the situation was finally recognized in the Beltway."

A Win for Apple

The exemptions cover¬Ýalmost $390 billion in U.S. imports, including more than $101 billion from China, Bloomberg reported, citing data compiled by¬ÝGerard DiPippo, associate director of the Rand China Research Center.

The biggest exempt category for Chinese goods is smartphones, which accounted for nearly $42 billion in U.S. imports in 2024.¬Ý

Although Apple (AAPL) has shifted some of its production to countries like India and Vietnam, it still assembles most of its devices in China.

It's¬Ýestimated that at least 50% of iPhone production comes from China, along with more than 50% of Macs and 75% to 80% of iPads.

Apple (AAPL) reportedly airlifted 600 tons of iPhones, or as many as 1.5 million, from India to the U.S., in an effort to beat the previously announced tariffs.

data.title: Trump tariffs stall bitcoin's rise now, but fuel long-term growth
data.description:
President Donald Trump's tariff moves have been about reasserting U.S. leverage in global trade, but the policy execution has been wildly aggressive and unpredictable, jolting the crypto market and virtually all other asset classes amid fears of trade wars and recession.¬Ý

Interestingly, bitcoin (BTC-USD) managed to hold steady last week above $80K, when the initial tariff announcement on April 2 roiled ex-crypto markets. At the time, the token's stability signaled that it may be living up to expectations of being a safe haven and independent of the mainstream financial system. But it didn't take long for bitcoin to catch up with the global rout,¬Ýdropping to below $75K just early this week.¬Ý

As the week powered on, Trump abruptly announced a tariff pause while significantly raising the levy on Chinese imports, a move that triggered a massive rebound in asset prices. Since then, bitcoin has been hovering above the $80K mark as uncertainty prevails, last changing hands at $82.3K as of Friday afternoon.

All told, bitcoin (BTC-USD) was down¬Ýless than 3% since "Liberation Day," faring better than the S&P 500's (SP500) 7.1% drop, as well as crude oil's 17% tumble and long-dated Treasury bonds' (TLT) 5.5% decline. Gold (XAUUSD:CUR) actually advanced 1.7%, while the U.S. dollar (DXY) was also off by¬Ýabout 3%.

"Bitcoin‚Äôs price volatility has increased by much less than equity volatility, and a range of indicators suggest that speculative trader positioning in crypto is relatively low," crypto asset manager Grayscale said in a research report. "If macro risks ease over the coming weeks, crypto valuations should be poised to rebound, in our ã‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äã‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äã‚Äå‚Äã‚Äã‚Äå‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äå‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äã‚Äã‚Äå‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äã‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äã‚Äå‚Äå‚Äå‚Äã‚Äå‚Äã‚Äã‚Äç‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äå‚Äã‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå‚Äç‚Äå‚Äã‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äå‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå‚Äã‚Äå‚Äã‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äå‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äå‚Äç‚Äå‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äã‚Äå‚Äå‚Äã‚Äã‚Äå‚Äå‚Äå‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äå‚Äç‚Äå‚Äã‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå"

What happens next over the near term will be dependent on trade discussions between Trump and other nations, Author Zach Pandl wrote. "Although negotiations could result in lower tariffs, setbacks in the talks could also result in more retaliation."

Even with Trump's 90-day tariff pause, the average effective U.S. tariff rate remains the highest in a century. Grayscale believes trade tensions -- namely with China -- and higher tariffs "should be considered positive for bitcoin adoption over the medium term." He cited stagflation risks induced by tariffs (that is, the simultaneous advance in inflation and fall in economic growth/employment) as a negative for traditional risk assets like equities, but a positive for scarce commodities such as gold (XAUUSD:USD).

Grayscale is in the camp that bitcoin (BTC-USD) is akin to digital hold, or a modern store of value in times of turmoil.

"Trade tensions may put pressure on reserve demand for the U.S. Dollar, opening space for competing assets, including other fiat currencies, gold, and bitcoin," the report said.

Conversely, in a Feb. 6 note, CoinShares analyst James Butterfill reckoned that tariffs would be negative for bitcoin (BTC-USD) over the short term, on the view that slower economic growth would temporarily reduce demand for risk assets.

"Unlike gold, Bitcoin has a growth component, meaning it reacts to economic trends and liquidity cycles," the note said.

data.title: Alphabet, Nvidia invest in AI startup Safe Superintelligence: report
data.description:
Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG)(NASDAQ:GOOGL) and Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) have invested in Safe Superintellgence, an AI startup co-founded by ex-OpenAI chief scientist Ilya Sutskever that launched last month, Reuters reported late Friday, citing a person familiar with the matter.¬Ý

According to the report, the size of the investments could not be determined.¬Ý

People reportedly said SSI was valued recently at $32B in a funding round headed by Greenoaks.¬Ý¬Ý

Safe Superintelligence, Alphabet, and Nvidia did not immediately respond to requests for comment by Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Executive reshuffles: CVS, NNDM, and DBX
data.description:
Market watchers, throughout the week, observed several Wall Street executives stepping up for new roles as part of leadership reshuffling.

Meanwhile, some companies such as CVS Health, Nano Dimension and Healthcare Realty added new positions to steer growth prospects.

Here are some of the recent management changes that took place during the week:

CVS Health (NYSE:CVS) finance chief Thomas Cowhey will step down amid the company's ongoing turnaround efforts led by CEO David Joyn. Brian Newman¬Ýhas been named executive vice president and CFO designate, effective April 21.

Nano Dimension (NASDAQ:NNDM) has named Ofir Baharav as its new CEO, effective immediately. In addition, Zivi Nedivi, president, and Tomer Pinchas, CFO and COO, are departing the company.

Dropbox (NASDAQ:DBX) has said that its Chief Customer Officer Eric Cox plans to step down.

Healthcare Realty (HR) has appointed¬ÝPeter Scott¬Ýas its CEO and president.¬ÝConnie Moore, who has served as interim CEO and president since November 2024, will transition out of that role and continue to serve on the board.

Golden Ocean (GOGL) has announced the¬Ýappointment¬Ýof Peder Simonsen as the CEO of Golden Ocean Management AS. Simonsen, who currently holds the roles of Interim CEO and CFO of Golden Ocean Management AS, will now take on the CEO position permanently.

BioCryst Pharmaceuticals (BCRX) said on Thursday that it has¬Ýappointed¬ÝJon P. Stonehouse, the company‚Äôs CEO, to serve as interim CFO and interim Principal Accounting Officer, effective as of the effective date.

CNH (CNH) has named Jim Nickolas as its next CFO, effective May 6, replacing Oddone Incisa.

Allison Transmission (ALSN) has announced the appointment of Scott Mell as its new CFO and treasurer, effective April 14, 2025.

data.title: Key deals this week: Capri, WNS Holdings, Fintech Payoneer, Altus Power and more
data.description:
Here's a list of key deals reported across sectors this week:

Capri Holdings (NYSE:CPRI) rose 8.3% amid reports that Prada (OTCPK:PRDSF) is close to acquiring the Versace brand for about $1.38 billion.

WNS Holdings (NYSE:WNS) rose 1.8% after a report that Capgemini SE (OTCPK:CAPMF) is in advanced discussions to purchase the outsourcing firm.

Fintech Payoneer Global (NASDAQ:PAYO) announced on Wednesday the close of the acquisition of a licensed China-based payment service provider, Easylink Payment.

Wellgistics Health (NASDAQ:WGRX) has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Peek Healthcare to expand its digital healthcare ecosystem.

PainReform (NASDAQ:PRFX) announced on Thursday the acquisition of DeepSolar, a provider of AI-driven software solutions designed to optimize solar energy usage in residential and utility-scale solar power plants.

Altus Power (NYSE:AMPS) said Wednesday that shareholders voted to approve the previously announced agreement for the company's acquisition by TPG through its TPG Rise Climate Transition Infrastructure strategy.

Ripple Labs, the company behind the XRP (XRP-USD) cryptocurrency, agreed to buy multi-asset prime brokerage firm Hidden Road for $1.25B, a deal that's expected to bolster Ripple's efforts on the stablecoin front, it said on Tuesday.

Germany's Infineon Technologies (OTCQX:IFNNY) agreed to purchase Marvell Technology's automotive ethernet business for about $2.5 billion in cash. Marvell¬Ýrose 3%¬Ýin after-hours trading.

Ki and Public Storage (NYSE:PSA) have submitted a non-binding indicative offer to acquire Abacus Storage King (ASK) for A$1.47 per stapled security. The acquisition targets all outstanding securities not already held by Ki Group, ASK's major security holder.

¬Ý

data.title: Trending stocks as Wall Street recovers from recent volatility
data.description:
After stocks tanked in the aftermath of US President Donald Trump‚Äôs tariff shocks last week, Wall Street seemed to have steadied itself and shook off some of the bearishness this week.

The S&P 500 jumped 7.8% over the week, similar to the Dow Jones Industrial Average. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite was up 9.2%.

Investors reacted positively to Trump issuing a 90-day tariff pause and Boston Fed President Susan Collins assuring that the Fed would ‚Äúabsolutely be prepared‚Äù to help stabilize financial markets if required.

However, caution still reigned in the market, with uncertainties around Trump‚Äôs rollercoaster trade policies and countries‚Äô reactions to them.

The week also saw the first quarter earnings season start with major U.S. banks releasing their latest quarterly reports.

Amid another wild week for Wall Street, these were some of the trending stocks:

US Steel (X) shares fell on Wednesday after President Donald Trump said he didn‚Äôt want a Japanese company to buy the Pittsburgh-based steelmaker. Earlier, on Monday, Trump ordered a national security review of its planned sale to Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY). The President also added later on Thursday that he likes Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) as an investor and doesn't want the U.S. steelmaker sold to a Japanese company.

CVS (CVS), Humana (HUM), UnitedHealth (UNH) and other managed-care stocks rose on Tuesday after the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services finalized a 5.06% average increase in payments to Medicare Advantage plans from 2025 to 2026, a sharp increase from the 2.23% the agency proposed as part of its Advance Notice in January.

Marvell Technology (MRVL) was in news after the chipmaker agreed to sell its automotive ethernet business for $2.5B in cash to Infineon Technologies.

Coal stocks gained on Tuesday as President Donald Trump signed executive orders intended to expand the production and use of coal in the U.S. Peabody Energy (BTU) rose 9.2%, Ramaco Resources (METC) +13.9%, Core Natural Resources (CNR) +6.6%, Warrior Met Coal (HCC) +6.3%, Alliance Resource Partners (ARLP) +4.8%, Alpha Metallurgical Resources (AMR) +2.4%, SunCoke Energy (SXC) +1.2%.

Easterly Government Properties (DEA) erased over 18% after the company announced a 32% cut to its quarterly dividend, lowering it to $0.18 per share from $0.265. The board also approved a 1-for-2.5 reverse stock split.

Trump Media (DJT) rose as much as 22.4% on Wednesday, notching its best gains since January this year. In a post on his social media platform Truth Social, Donald Trump said, "THIS IS A GREAT TIME TO BUY!!! DJT." The post came hours before the announcement of the 90-day pause on the U.S. reciprocal tariffs unveiled by him earlier this month.

Shares in companies working on biotech AI models gained after the FDA said on Thursday it plans to phase out animal testing requirements for monoclonal antibodies and other drugs. Recursion Pharmaceuticals (RXRX), Absci (ABSI), Certara (CERT), Schrodinger (SDGR) and Nuvation Bio (NUVB) were some of the notable gainers.

Shares of major European drugmakers, including AstraZeneca (AZN), GSK (GSK), Novartis (NVS), Sanofi (SNY) and Novo Nordisk (NVO), fell Wednesday morning after U.S. President Donald Trump reaffirmed plans to impose a "major" tariff on all pharmaceutical imports.
